blocks.text:
A ball is released from rest and falls $h = 20\ \text{m}$ to the ground. Taking
$g = 9.8\ \text{m/s}^2$ and neglecting air resistance, find its speed $v$ at impact.

Turn on **Quiz mode** to check your own answer.

blocks.source:
point A at=(0,3) label="A" dot=true
point B at=(0,0) label="B" dot=true
line from=(0,3) to=(0,0) style=dashed
vector from=(0,1.4) to=(0,0.4) label="v"
ground at=(-1,0) width=2
dim from=(0.55,3) to=(0.55,0) off=0.0 label="h"

meta.solution:
Energy conservation gives $\tfrac{1}{2}v^2 = gh$, so $v = \sqrt{2gh}$. With $g = 9.8\,$m/s²
and $h = 20\,$m, $v = \sqrt{2\cdot 9.8\cdot 20} \approx 19.8\,$m/s.
meta.subject: physics
meta.title: Free Fall — Impact Speed
